emails.subject: Conoco Contract Nos. 20747 & 20748
emails.body: Jeanette: I just reviewed the handout for your presentation on the Global Settlement. One of your slides stated that the TCR surcharge is applicable for 10 years from the Global settlement, even if the CFS's contract expires. Conoco Contract Nos. 20747 20748 expired at the end of February. I assume we should bill the TCR surcharge for these contracts beginning in March, 2002. Please let me know if this is correct. Thanks! Jan Moore X53858

emails.subject: FW: Conoco Contract Nos. 20747 & 20748
emails.body: FW: Conoco Contract Nos. 20747 20748 We still need to bill Conoco the TCR II Surcharges on 20,000 MMBtu per day. Maybe Michelle can get with Conoco and see how they want it billed. If they want it billed on their new contract, maybe we can do an amendment. If they want it billed on the old contracts, then you need to contact someone in the contract group to see how we bill it since the contracts are expired (i.e., overriding the CBS system or something). Jeanette

emails.subject: RE: Conoco Contract Nos. 20747 & 20748
emails.body: RE: Conoco Contract Nos. 20747 20748 The TCR II surcharges are only charged on 20747 and 20748, the mainline contracts, not 20835 (it is a SJ contract).
